subject: Chemistry
title: Isaac Newton: The Secret Alchemist
description: A short lesson to help you apply Isaac Newton: The Secret Alchemist.

hook.text: Isaac Newton wrote more about alchemy than physics. He spent 30 years trying to make the philosopher's stone.

buildup.text: After publishing the Principia, Newton spent most of his time in a private lab doing secret alchemical experiments.

discovery.text: His alchemical notes fill over a million words—more than all his physics and math writings combined.

twist.text: Newton kept it secret because alchemy was becoming disreputable. Revealing it could have ruined his reputation.

climax.text: His hair contained mercury at 40 times the normal level. His famous mental breakdown may have been mercury poisoning.

punchline.text: The father of physics spent more time on alchemy. His secret obsession.

quiz.question: Why did Newton keep his alchemical work secret?
options: Alchemy was becoming disreputable and could have ruined his reputation
He was afraid someone would steal his gold-making recipe
The Church had banned all alchemical research
